questionText: Provide any efficiency metrics that are appropriate for your organization's transport products and/or services.
questionKo: any efficiency metrics that are appropriate for 귀사의 transport products 및/또는 services.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Efficiency metrics are the primary way through which transport sector companies can measure the energy efficiency of their vehicles across modes of transportation. Various primary metrics exist as standards for different modes of transport, and sector experts and relevant data users will be able to use this information to compare the company's overall efficiency.

guidance.text:
General 
Metrics reported, using the associated metric numerators and denominators (presented in columns 3 and 4, respectively), should be for the current reporting period (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 
When reporting "% change from last year" (column 7), the timeline used should be the 12-month period directly prior to the reporting period (as defined by your answer to 1.4), even if it does not completely overlap with the period previously reported to CDP. 
Aside from in "% change from last year field" (column 7), negative numbers should not be inserted. 
Activity (column 1) 
Select the activity that you would like to provide data for. 
Activity modes presented in drop-down options are determined by transport modes selected in response to 1.21. 
Metric figure (column 2) 
Enter the numerical value used to quantify the efficiency metric appropriate for your organization's products and/or services pertinent to the metric numerator (column 3) and metric denominator (column 4) within your transport activities selected in column 1. 
Metrics reported for each of the transport activities that your organization engages and/or operates in can be in relation to fuel, distance, financial and any other metric denominator(s) deemed relevant for the transport mode(s) that your organization operate(s) in. Further explanation of these metrics can be provided in columns 3, 4 and 8 (Metric numerator, Metric denominator and Please explain, respectively). 
Metric numerator (column 3) 
Select relevant metric numerator. 
If you select "Other, please specify", provide a label for the Metric numerator. 
Metric denominator (column 4) 
Select relevant metric denominator. 
If you select "Other, please specify", provide a label for the Metric denominator. 
If reporting marine freight in container size-distance equivalents (e.g. 20ft.km), please explain your payload assumptions in column 8 (Please explain). 
Metric numerator: Unit total (column 5) 
Please enter the numerical value of the numerator selected in column 3, used to evaluate the metric figure presented (column 2), for the transport activity selected (column 1). 
Metric denominator: Unit total (column 6) 
Enter the numerical value of the denominator selected in column 4, used to evaluate the metric figure presented (column 2), for the transport activity selected (column 1). 
% change from last year (column 7) 
Enter the percentage change in the efficiency metric you are reporting on (column 2), in relation to the previous year. 
Leave the column blank if you do not have sufficient data to calculate the change from the previous year, or if this is the first year you have tracked this metric. 
If you have experienced no change, please enter 0 (zero) in this column. 
Please explain (column 8). 
Discuss any assumptions made to derive, or simplifications made to establish metric numerators and denominators used. 
If you used any relevant existing standards and/or methodologies, please mention them and discuss their use here. 
Provide any additional explanation necessary to capture the full complexity of the efficiency metric stated. 
Note for marine sector 
You are encouraged to report your EEDI attainment ratio, which serves as an indicator of the overall efficiency of your fleet. EEDI attainment ratio is the proportion of ships in your fleet that achieved minimum EEDI. 
To report this metric, select "Other, please specify" in both column 3 (Metric numerator) and column 4 (Metric denominator) and using the text field provided enter "Number of ships achieved minimum EEDI" for the numerator, and "Total number of ships in the fleet" for denominator. Enter the respective numerical values in columns 5 (Metric numerator: Unit total) and column 6 (Metric denominator: Unit total).
guidance.textKo: 일반 Metrics 보고, using the associated metric numerators 및 denominators (presented in 열 3 및 4, respectively), 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 current reporting period (as defined by your answer to 1.4). 경우 reporting "% change from last year" (열 7), the timeline used 해야 합니다 be the 12-month period directly prior to the reporting period (as defined by your answer to 1.4), even 만약 it does not completely overlap with the period previously 보고 to CDP. Aside from in "% change from last year field" (열 7), negative numbers 해야 합니다 not be inserted. Activity (열 1) Select the activity that you would like to provide data for. Activity modes presented in drop-down options are determined by transport modes 선택 in response to 1.21. Metric figure (열 2) Enter the numerical value used to quantify the efficiency metric appropriate for 귀사의 products 및/또는 services pertinent to the metric numerator (열 3) 및 metric denominator (열 4) within your transport activities 선택 in 열 1. Metrics 보고 for each 의 transport activities that 귀사 engages 및/또는 operates in can be in relation to fuel, distance, financial 및 하나 이상 other metric denominator(s) deemed 관련 에 대한 transport mode(s) that 귀사 operate(s) in. Further 설명 의se metrics can be 입력 in 열 3, 4 및 8 (Metric numerator, Metric denominator 및 Please explain, respectively). Metric numerator (열 3) Select 관련 metric numerator. 만약 you select "Other, 직접 입력", provide a label 에 대한 Metric numerator. Metric denominator (열 4) Select 관련 metric denominator. 만약 you select "Other, 직접 입력", provide a label 에 대한 Metric denominator. 만약 reporting marine freight in container size-distance equivalents (e.g. 20ft.km), please explain your payload assumptions in 열 8 (Please explain). Metric numerator: Unit total (열 5) Please enter the numerical value 의 numerator 선택 in 열 3, used to evaluate the metric figure presented (열 2), 에 대한 transport activity 선택 (열 1). Metric denominator: Unit total (열 6) Enter the numerical value 의 denominator 선택 in 열 4, used to evaluate the metric figure presented (열 2), 에 대한 transport activity 선택 (열 1). % change from last year (열 7) Enter the percentage change in the efficiency metric you are reporting on (열 2), in relation to the previous year. Leave the 열 blank 만약 you do not have sufficient data to calculate the change from the previous year, 또는 만약 this is the first year you have tracked this metric. 만약 you have experienced no change, please enter 0 (zero) in this 열. Please explain (열 8). Discuss 하나 이상 assumptions made to derive, 또는 simplifications made to establish metric numerators 및 denominators used. 만약 you used 하나 이상 관련 existing 기준 및/또는 methodologies, please mention them 및 discuss their use here. Provide 하나 이상 additional 설명 necessary to capture the full complexity 의 efficiency metric stated. Note for marine sector You are encouraged to report your EEDI attainment ratio, which serves as an indicator 의 overall efficiency of your fleet. EEDI attainment ratio is the proportion of ships in your fleet that achieved 최소 EEDI. To report this metric, select "Other, 직접 입력" in both 열 3 (Metric numerator) 및 열 4 (Metric denominator) 및 using the text field 입력 enter "Number of ships achieved 최소 EEDI" 에 대한 numerator, 및 "Total number of ships in the fleet" for denominator. Enter the respective numerical values in 열 5 (Metric numerator: Unit total) 및 열 6 (Metric denominator: Unit total).

guidance.text:
Available Seat Kilometer (ASK): the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in kilometers.
Available Seat Mile (ASM): the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in miles.
Cubic meter-kilometer (m3.km): the transportation of one cubic meter of load, cargo or freight by a given transport mode over a distance of one kilometer.
Cubic meter-mile (m3.mile): the transportation of one cubic meter of load, cargo or freight by a given transport mode over a distance of one mile.
Cubic meter-nautical mile (m3.nautical mile): the transportation of one cubic meter of load, cargo or freight by a given transport mode over a distance of one nautical mile.
Energy Efficiency Design Index (EEDI): as defined by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) , EEDI represents the amount of CO 2 generated by a ship while doing one ton-mile of transport work.
Energy Efficiency Design Index (EEDI) Attainment Ratio: a fleet-wide metric derived by dividing the total number of ships that achieved minimum EEDI by the total number of ships in the fleet.
Kilogram vehicle mass (kg vehicle mass): the transportation of one kilogram of vehicle mass by a defined mode of transport.
Kilogram vehicle mass-kilometer (kg vehicle mass.km): the transportation of one kilogram of vehicle mass by a defined mode of transport over the distance of one kilometer.
Kilogram vehicle mass-mile (kg vehicle mass.mile): the transportation of one kilogram of vehicle mass by a defined mode of transport over the distance of one mile.
m2 vehicle footprint: the product surface area of the average track width (distance between the centerline of the tires) and the wheelbase (the distance between the centers of the axles); quantified in m2.
MWh (megawatt hours): one MWh is the equivalent of 1 x 10 6 Watts of energy being used continuously for one hour, equivalent to the consumption of 3.6 GJ (3.6 x 10 9 joules). Therefore, to convert from gigajoules (GJ) to MWh, you should multiply your value by 0.277778.
Passenger-kilometer (p.km): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one passenger by a defined mode of transport over a distance of one kilometer.
Passenger-mile (p.mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one passenger by a defined mode of transport over a distance of one mile.
Passenger-nautical mile (p.nautical mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one passenger by a defined mode of transport over a distance of one nautical mile.
Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK): This metric evaluates operating income by the ASK (Available Seat Kilometer). ASK is the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in kilometers. 
Revenue per Available Seat Mile (RASM): This metric evaluates operating income by the ASM (Available Seat Mile). ASM is the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in miles.
Revenue ton-kilometer (revenue-ton.km): a metric ton of revenue load transported over one kilometer.
Revenue ton-mile (revenue-ton.mile): a metric ton of revenue load transported over one mile.
Revenue ton-nautical mile (revenue-ton.nautical mile): a metric ton of revenue load transported over one nautical mile.
Ton-kilometer (t.km): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (including packaging and tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one kilometer.
Ton-mile (t.mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (including packaging and tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one mile.
Ton-nautical mile (t.nautical mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (including packaging and tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one nautical mile.
Vehicle-kilometer (vehicle.km or v.km): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vehicles on a given road or traffic network, over a distance of one kilometer (loaded or empty).
Vehicle-mile (vehicle.mile or v.mile): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vehicles on a given road or traffic network, over a distance of one mile (loaded or empty).
Vessel-nautical mile (v.nautical mile): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vessels on a given route or traffic network, over a distance of one nautical mile (loaded or empty).
20 foot container-kilometer (20ft.km): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one kilometer.
20 foot container-mile (20ft.mile): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one mile.
20 foot container-nautical mile (20ft.nautical mile): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one nautical mile.
40 foot container-kilometer (40ft.km): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one kilometer.
40 foot container-mile (40ft.mile): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one mile.
40 foot container-nautical mile (40ft.nautical mile): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one nautical mile.

Available Seat Mile (ASM): the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in miles. Cubic meter-kilometer (m3.km): the transportation of one cubic meter of load, cargo 또는 freight by a given transport mode over a distance of one kilometer. Cubic meter-nautical mile (m3.nautical mile): the transportation of one cubic meter of load, cargo 또는 freight by a given transport mode over a distance of one nautical mile. Energy Efficiency Design Index (EEDI): as defined by the International Maritime Organization (IMO), EEDI represents the amount of CO 2 generated by a ship while doing one ton-mile of transport work. Energy Efficiency Design Index (EEDI) Attainment Ratio: a fleet-wide metric derived by dividing the total number of ships that achieved 최소 EEDI by the total number of ships in the fleet. Kilogram vehicle mass (kg vehicle mass): the transportation of one kilogram of vehicle mass by a defined mode of transport. Kilogram vehicle mass-kilometer (kg vehicle mass.km): the transportation of one kilogram of vehicle mass by a defined mode of transport over the distance of one kilometer. MWh (megawatt hours): one MWh is the equivalent of 1 x 10 6 Watts of energy being used continuously for one hour, equivalent to the consumption of 3.6 GJ (3.6 x 10 9 joules). Therefore, to convert from gigajoules (GJ) to MWh, you 해야 합니다 multiply your value by 0.277778. Passenger-mile (p.mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one passenger by a defined mode of transport over a distance of one mile. Passenger-nautical mile (p.nautical mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one passenger by a defined mode of transport over a distance of one nautical mile. 매출 per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK): This metric evaluates operating income by the ASK (Available Seat Kilometer). ASM is the number of seats available multiplied by the distance flown in miles. 매출 ton-kilometer (매출-ton.km): a metric ton of 매출 load transported over one kilometer. 매출 ton-mile (매출-ton.mile): a metric ton of 매출 load transported over one mile. 매출 ton-nautical mile (매출-ton.nautical mile): a metric ton of 매출 load transported over one nautical mile. Ton-kilometer (t.km): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (포함 packaging 및 tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one kilometer. Ton-mile (t.mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (포함 packaging 및 tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one mile. Ton-nautical mile (t.nautical mile): a unit of measurement which represents the transportation of one metric ton of goods (포함 packaging 및 tare weights of intermodal transport units), by a given transport mode over a distance of one nautical mile. Vehicle-kilometer (vehicle.km 또는 v.km): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vehicles on a given road 또는 traffic network, over a distance of one kilometer (loaded 또는 empty). Vehicle-mile (vehicle.mile 또는 v.mile): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vehicles on a given road 또는 traffic network, over a distance of one mile (loaded 또는 empty). Vessel-nautical mile (v.nautical mile): a unit of measurement for traffic flow which represents the number of vessels on a given 경로 또는 traffic network, over a distance of one nautical mile (loaded 또는 empty). 20 foot container-kilometer (20ft.km): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one kilometer. 20 foot container-mile (20ft.mile): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one mile. 20 foot container-nautical mile (20ft.nautical mile): the transportation of a 20ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one nautical mile. 40 foot container-kilometer (40ft.km): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one kilometer. 40 foot container-mile (40ft.mile): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one mile. 40 foot container-nautical mile (40ft.nautical mile): the transportation of a 40ft shipping container by a defined mode of transport, over a distance of one nautical mile.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed cell in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 7/7 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성된 칸(셀) in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 7/7점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 4 points
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question.

simpleHelpKo: Efficiency metrics are the primary way through which transport sector companies can measure the energy efficiency 의ir vehicles across modes of transportation. Various primary metrics exist as 기준 for different modes of transport, 및 sector experts 및 관련 데이터 사용자 will be able to use this information to compare the company's overall efficiency.
